Social news is a daily feature OF
The Rress. Any one wishing to in
sert Shch news should mail it to the
society editor or phone before ~
q. m.


The women of the federated w. c.
T. U. will be hostesses this evening
at a party to be given at the Y. w.
c. A. parlors complimentary to all
young woman who are employed in
the various hotels in the city.


Miss Natalia Soules, one of the
secretaries of the Y. w. G. A., is
spending a few weeks at Kalispell,
Mont.


A pretty, quiet wedding was sol
emnized this morning at the church
of Our Lady of Lourdes. The prin-
cipals in the pretty ceremony were
Miss Margaret Tracy, Forest City,
Mo-, and Fred Birney of Spokane.
tn service was read by Rev.
Father Verhagen in the presence of
a few intimate friends. The bride
was attended by Miss Anna Tracy.
c. p. Robbins served as best man.
Mr.. and Mrs. Birney will visit the
brides parents in the east and will
then go abroad.


3 4 =


Miss Lucy Mccoy and Ernest A.
Cameron were married yesterday
morning at the st. Aloysius church.
Rev. Father George Butler read the
service. The bride was attended by
Miss Molly O'Donnell. James Her.
rick was best man. The young
couple left Immediately for we'd
ding trip to the coast.


Z & &


The meeting of the Spokane chap
ter of the American WOman's
league held last night at the library
was we'll attended and the members
showed great enthusiasm when it
was announced that as result OF
their recent campaign that the
chapter now contains 205 members.
Mr.. w. h. Goldthwait and Miss
Colborn spoke at length about the
sites for the chapter house. Miss
Hunnicut, president Of the club,
read important communications
from headquarters concerning the
same subject. 1t Is expected more
definite arrangements will be made
within the next six weeks.


= 2 4


The Reno circle of the Women of
Woodcraft will entertain their mem;
bers and friends at a card party
Friday evening in the lower Pacific
halls. Whist will be the game
played.


Mrs. r. c. Kronick received the
ladies of the Degree of Honor So
cial club informally this afternoon
at her home on South Oak street.
Mrs. Kronick is not member Of
the club. but having been honor
guest on a number of occasions, she
returned the compliment by enter
tanning in most charming manner
this afternoon.


& & &


The members of the Women's
Missionary society of the West
minister Congregational church are
guests today of Mrs. Louis Thomp-
son.


The graduation exercises of the
Spokane college will be held Sat
urday evening in the college gym
naSium.


Tuesday afternoon. June Il. the
nurses of the st. Luke's hospital
will hold their commencement ex-
erCiscS at the parish house of All
Saints cathedral. At this time 10
young ladies will receive diplomas.
On Tuesday evening reception
will be given the graduating class
in the Hall of the Doges, Daven-
ports.
